issues.title: Checkpoint string truncation corrupts resumed workflow state
issues.problem: saveWorkflowCheckpoint passes context.nodes and context.artifacts through sanitizeCheckpointValue, which truncates every string longer than 10,000 characters. On resume, restoreWorkflowCheckpoint treats those sanitized results as successfully completed nodes and reuses them verbatim. Downstream nodes consume artifacts such as {{artifacts.change}} (which contains filesWithDiffs[].patch strings) and {{artifacts.review}}, so a resumed run can operate on incomplete diffs or findings and produce wrong review/fix results.

issues.title: Shared activeNodeId can record the wrong node in checkpoint failure metadata
issues.problem: Both the static wave executor and the DAG-segment executor set executionContext.activeNodeId = nodeId inside Promise.allSettled callbacks that run concurrently. The failure-handling code at lines 3505 and 3544 reads this shared mutable field to populate failure.nodeId. When a wave has multiple runnable nodes, the last node to start overwrites the field, so the checkpoint can attribute the failure to a node that did not fail. The DAG-segment catch block also records no nodeId of its own and relies on the same shared state in the outer catch.
